agent_analysis.hypothesis_rationale: Higher fundraising expenses increase donation returns which subsequently expands total expenditure capability.
agent_analysis.prior_art_notes: Bielefeld (1992) analyzes the structural scale of nonprofits and the link between overhead and program expenditures.
agent_analysis.interpretation: A statistically significant positive correlation was found. Nonprofits spending more on fundraising experience higher total expenses, demonstrating that fundraising successfully drives scaling.
